title: Your Meridian Role Is Now 'Sales'
body: Meridian now has four distinct roles instead of three generic ones. Your login (bd_team / meridian2026) is now the 'sales' role, which gives you full access to outreach, CRM, and pipeline tools. Management users (like board members) get read-only CRM access. You won't notice much difference day-to-day — you still see everything you need.

title: Operations Module Live in Meridian
body: Jonathon now has his own Operations section in Meridian for tracking credentialing, contracts, activation checklists, and client health. You won't see this section (it's gated to operations and admin), but it means credentialing status and client activation progress are now tracked in one place instead of scattered across spreadsheets.

title: Org Research Now Visible in Meridian
body: When you open an org profile in Meridian, there's now a Research tab showing pain signals, messaging angles, and discovered contacts. The dashboard also has a research pipeline widget. This is the same intelligence that drives ABM email personalization — now you can see it before a call or meeting.

title: New Client Testimonial Added to Website
body: A testimonial from a COO at Shasta Community Health Center (Baynard) has been added to the homepage carousel, the For Health Centers page, and the Executive landing page. The existing Scheuber testimonial was also revised based on provider feedback. When you share these pages with prospects, they'll now see an additional real client voice.

title: Two Blog Posts Fixed — Had Incomplete Text
body: The '9 Health Centers, One Question' and 'Doc in the Box' blog posts were live with placeholder text instead of finished content. Both are now corrected with complete copy. Safe to share these links again.

title: Portal Expansion: Mobile, Objections, Partner Context, Language Update
body: Mobile layout fixed (hamburger nav, scrollable tables). Objection handling expanded to 4 personas (CEO, BH Director, Medical Director, Finance). Partner reference points added. Elevator pitches added. Financial language updated per Jonathon's feedback: 'cash generated to serve your mission' replaces 'mission cash generated.' Revenue qualifier added ($230 is an average, PPS rates vary $180-$400+). Additional competitor comparisons added.

title: BD Team Portal Now Live
body: Your new team portal is live at GoLegara.com/team (password: legara-team-2026). It has everything in one place: current messaging, competitive positioning, key numbers, objection handling, sales process, and this changelog. Check your email from Roger for the full intro.

title: Partners Page Rewritten with Deployment Models
body: The Partners page now shows four real deployment archetypes (Scale, Rural Access, Backfill, Language Access) instead of fictional partner profiles. Use these when prospects ask 'who else does this?' — each model maps to a common FQHC scenario.

title: Assessment Results Page Enhanced
body: Prospects who complete the assessment now get tier-specific next steps ('What Organizations at Your Level Typically Do Next'), a results email, and explore links. The experience is significantly better — they get immediate value, not just a form fill. If a prospect mentions their score or tier in conversation, you can reference the same framework.

title: Client-Facing Page Copy Cleaned Up
body: Removed internal jargon ('ramp') from several client-facing pages and fixed a factual error on the Clinical landing page. Blog posts now cross-link to each other. Nothing you need to do differently — just cleaner pages when you share links.

title: Messaging Pivot Complete
body: All external content now leads with structural differentiation, not financial savings. The narrative arc is: Recognition → Understanding → Proof → Financial Validation. Financial messaging is deemphasized, not eliminated. 'Mission cash generated' stays as a phrase, but arrives 4th in the conversation, not 1st.

title: Timeline Claims Corrected
body: Two timeline claims were corrected across all materials. Legara provider placement: 'as fast as 6 weeks from signed contract' (was '5 months'). Internal hire ramp: '6-9 months from hire to full caseload' (was '11 months'). Both independently verified with published AAPPR benchmarking data. These are the only approved phrasings going forward.

title: Assessment Replaces Calculator
body: The ROI calculator is no longer public-facing. It's now a selective sales tool Roger uses in live CFO walkthroughs. The primary conversion tool is the Behavioral Health Capacity Assessment at GoLegara.com/assessment. All CTAs updated across site, emails, and collateral.

title: Brevo Email Sequences Updated
body: Sequence A (post-assessment nurture, 4 emails) and Sequence B (meeting confirmation, 2 emails) rebuilt with post-pivot content. Templates now use assessment framing, three-pillar education arc, and correct variable syntax.

title: Blog Posts Rewritten
body: 'The 11-Month Blind Spot' retitled to 'The Ramp Cost Blind Spot' with concurrent timeline model. '$229K Therapist' blog updated with corrected CPE math.

title: Assessment Page Live
body: BH Capacity Index assessment deployed at GoLegara.com/assessment. 7-dimension scoring, 4 tiers, integrated with HubSpot and Brevo. Takes prospects ~3 minutes. Replaces the calculator as primary conversion path.

title: ABM Campaign Launched
body: Daily outreach campaign running. 8-12 orgs/day, multi-stakeholder, CEO-to-CEO positioning. ~125 emails across 86 contacts in first 4 days. Differentiation-first messaging across all role angles.
